Caught this post early in the day and it really threw me off a little. Paper flower making, imo, is really about slowing down, with each petal shaped carefully to bring a breath of realism and personality into every bloom. It is possible for paper flowers to be mass produced and still retain some realism, but honestly it is hard to see that spark of life in these IKEA ones. They are quite literally paper flowers, as marketed. What has not been mentioned is that most of these blooms are made with crepe paper, which is such a beautiful material with its natural stretch and texture. It is meant to be shaped and worked with intention, not just treated like ordinary paper. For those based in Singapore and are curious to explore what paper can truly become, I do host paper flower workshops where you get to learn the art of paper flowers.
